name: Caponata (Eggplant Appetizer)
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 lb Unpeeled eggplant, cut into 3/4-inch cubes
4 Med. sweet red peppers, cut into 3/4-inch squares (2-1/2 cups)
8 Med. ripe Italian tomatoes, pureed to make 2 cups
recipeInstructions:
1. Sprinkle the eggplant with coarse salt and toss to coat. Let it drain in a colander for 30 minutes, then pat dry with paper towels.
2. Preheat oven to 375°F.
3. Heat olive oil in an 12-inch skillet over medium-high heat. Saute onion, peppers, and celery for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
4. Add the eggplant cubes and toss for another 5 minutes.
5. Add the tomato puree, red wine vinegar, sugar, and garlic. Stir for 2 more minutes.
6. If using an ovenproof skillet, transfer to the oven and bake uncovered for 20 minutes. If using a baking dish, place in the oven and bake uncovered for 20 minutes.
7. Add the olives and capers, stirring well. Bake for another 15 minutes or until most of the liquid has evaporated.
8. Let the caponata cool completely. Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper. Refrigerate, covered, for at least 24 hours before serving. Caponata will keep in the refrigerator for at least a week, or frozen for up to 6 months.
